articles.summary: The race for the final IPL playoffs spot intensifies. Rajasthan Royals (14 points) must win their last match against MI to guarantee qualification. Kolkata Knight Riders (13 points) need to beat Delhi Capitals by a significant margin to overtake Punjab Kings on NRR, provided RR loses. Both teams face crucial showdowns today to secure their spot.

articles.headline: F1: Russell Aims to Recapture Momentum, Stop Antonelli's Streak
articles.summary: George Russell seeks to regain momentum and halt Kimi Antonelli's winning streak at the Canadian Grand Prix. Russell's fourth place in Miami left him 20 points behind the 19-year-old Italian, who has won three consecutive races. Russell admitted focusing too much on the 2026 cars' electrical power output and neglecting fundamentals led to losing ground, serving as a 'painful reminder.'

articles.headline: Enhanced Games: Las Vegas to Host 'All Drug Olympics'
articles.summary: The 'Enhanced Games,' featuring 50 athletes free to use performance enhancers, is set for Sunday in Las Vegas with track, swimming, and weightlifting events. Touted as a 'paradigm-shifting' event, it aims to challenge mainstream anti-doping efforts and highlight potential medical advancements for human performance. Backed by Peter Thiel, it offers $250,000 prizes and $1 million bonuses for world records, though these won't count in official record books.

articles.headline: Govt orders Delhi Gymkhana to vacate premises by June 5
articles.summary: The Union government ordered Delhi Gymkhana Club to vacate its 27.3-acre premises by June 5, citing a "public purpose clause" and strategic area needs for defence and public security. This follows a long-standing governance dispute and NCLT action.

articles.headline: 12 more plants to clean sewage on Najafgarh drain
articles.summary: DJB has awarded work for 12 decentralized sewage treatment plants in Najafgarh to improve sewage management and clean the Yamuna. The Najafgarh drain contributes 60-70% of pollution to the river.

articles.headline: Heatwave to grip parts of India till month-end
articles.summary: IMD forecasts heatwave to severe heatwave conditions over central and northwestern India till month-end, with isolated rainfall likely over peninsular India. Telangana reported 16 suspected heat stroke deaths this season.

articles.headline: Kerala govt faces heat on Kelkar appointment
articles.summary: Kerala's government faces criticism from BJP and CPI(M) over the appointment of former CEO Rathan U Kelkar as CM's secretary. Critics allege hypocrisy and "planned interference" during elections. Congress defends it as the government's prerogative.

articles.headline: SATHEESAN MEETS SONIA, RAHUL IN FIRST DELHI VISIT AS KERALA CM
articles.summary: Kerala Chief Minister V D Satheesan met top Congress leaders Sonia Gandhi, Rahul Gandhi, and Mallikarjun Kharge during his first Delhi visit. Discussions focused on organizational matters and the functioning of the new UDF government.

articles.headline: B’luru boxing coach booked after minor alleges sexual harassment
articles.summary: Bengaluru police launched a criminal probe against a 50-year-old boxing coach for alleged sexual harassment of a 17-year-old female athlete. The coach is accused of misconduct over four months, threatening the victim.

articles.headline: 5 killed as car, lorry collide in Kalaburagi
articles.summary: Five people were killed in Kalaburagi after a cruiser vehicle collided head-on with a lorry on National Highway 150 near Ladlapur village late Friday night.

articles.headline: ANDHRA TO START DIGITAL HEALTH MISSION ON AUG 15
articles.summary: Andhra Pradesh CM N Chandrababu Naidu announced that "Sanjeevani," a health record digitization and services program, will launch statewide on August 15. The mission will monitor health aspects and offer "AI doctor" services soon.

articles.headline: Elephant runs amok in Thrissur, damages vehicles
articles.summary: An elephant ran amok in a residential area in Thrissur city, damaging vehicles and properties, causing panic. Nobody was injured. The elephant became agitated while returning from a temple.

articles.headline: IMD issues orange alert in four Kerala districts
articles.summary: The IMD issued an orange alert for Thrissur, Kollam, Kottayam, and Idukki districts for three hours from 4 pm, forecasting thunderstorms with heavy rainfall and gusty winds as monsoon approaches Kerala.

articles.headline: Cops took independent call to deny nod for CJP event: Min
articles.summary: Karnataka Home Minister G Parameshwara said police independently denied permission for a proposed "Cockroach Janata Party" (CJP) event in Bengaluru, based on law and order assessment, dismissing claims of political interference.

articles.headline: Shivakumar cautions voters on SIR
articles.summary: Karnataka Deputy CM D K Shivakumar warned of a "big conspiracy" to remove poor voters' rights via the Special Intensive Revision (SIR) process, urging everyone to apply for voting rights before the October 7 final electoral roll.

articles.headline: TN village throws 100th birthday bash for resident
articles.summary: Mallammal, a woman from the Badagas community in Nilgiris district, turned 100, celebrated by her village and family, who claim she is the first centenarian from the community.

articles.headline: Siddaramaiah lays foundation stone for international cricket stadium in B’luru
articles.summary: Karnataka CM Siddaramaiah laid the foundation for an international cricket stadium in Suryanagar, Anekal taluk. The ₹943 crore project, a joint initiative, will have 80,000 seating, making it India's second largest.

articles.headline: Heat wave to continue grip over central India till May end: IMD
articles.summary: IMD forecasts heat wave to severe heat wave conditions across central India till month-end, with isolated rainfall in peninsular India. Temperatures up to 47.2°C recorded. El Nino conditions expected to develop in June-August.

articles.headline: 3 dead in Odisha due to heatwave
articles.summary: At least three people have died in Odisha due to heatwave-related incidents this month. The deaths were reported from Bolangir, Sundergarh, and Sonepur districts, as large parts of the state continued to reel under hot conditions.

articles.headline: House officials to get EV cars as part of ‘save fuel’ initiative
articles.summary: Parliament is replacing petrol-run official vehicles of senior House officers with EVs to cut fuel costs, following PM Modi's appeal. At least 40 officers are expected to receive Mahindra XUV 400 or Tata Nexon EV models.

articles.headline: BENGAL REVISES HOLIDAY SCHEDULE FOR EID AL-ADHA
articles.summary: The West Bengal government declared May 28 as a public holiday for Eid al-Adha, cancelling previous holidays on May 26 and 27. The order specified these days would now be working days.

articles.headline: WB gets over ₹3k-cr for health care
articles.summary: West Bengal CM Suvendu Adhikari announced the Centre sanctioned ₹3,000 crore for revamping the state's public healthcare sector. Ayushman Bharat health insurance scheme is expected to roll out in the state from July.

articles.headline: RG Kar case: Govt begins proceedings against suspended doctor
articles.summary: The Bengal government initiated departmental proceedings against Dr. Avik De, suspended after a rape and murder at RG Kar Medical College and Hospital. A separate inquiry into his postgraduate admission was also ordered.

articles.headline: ED tightens grip on ex-Bengal cop in land grab case
articles.summary: The Enforcement Directorate initiated attachment proceedings for a bungalow belonging to former Kolkata Police deputy commissioner Santanu Sinha Biswas in Murshidabad, following his arrest in a land grab and fraud case.

articles.headline: ‘India to be biggest arms exporter in 25-30 years’
articles.summary: Defence minister Rajnath Singh stated India, once a weapons importer, will become the biggest arms exporter in 25-30 years. He highlighted increased private sector participation and the goal to reach 50% contribution in defence manufacturing.

articles.headline: Naga abduction case to be handed over to NIA
articles.summary: Manipur CM Yumnam Khemchand Singh announced that the case of six abducted Naga villagers would be handed over to the NIA. He also appealed to Kuki groups to help release 14 Kuki civilians in captivity.

articles.headline: Delhi CM reviews new tech to curb localised air pollution
articles.summary: Delhi CM Rekha Gupta inspected new Indian-made technologies to tackle air pollution, including filter-free air purifiers and EV-mounted anti-smog guns. She stressed a year-round commitment to clean air, despite expert skepticism on some devices.

articles.headline: Sub-registrar offices set for facelift on lines of passport offices
articles.summary: The Delhi government plans to overhaul sub-registrar offices, transforming them into modern, technology-driven service centers similar to Passport Seva Kendras. Services will include online booking, live tracking, and advanced security.

articles.headline: Two techies die in fire, crash on Ggm roads
articles.summary: Two engineers died in separate road accidents in Gurugram. One crashed into a crane near Sikanderpur Metro, while another's speeding car caught fire after hitting an auto-rickshaw near IFFCO Chowk.

articles.headline: Chhath Ghat near Munak Canal
articles.summary: Delhi CM Rekha Gupta inspected the under-construction Chhath Ghat near Munak Canal, directing officials to ensure arrangements for convenience, safety, and cleanliness for devotees. She emphasized government commitment to religious and cultural sites.

articles.headline: 19 get life term in ’16 Odisha violence
articles.summary: A sessions court sentenced 19 people, including a former BJD functionary, to life imprisonment for a 2016 triple murder and arson case in Odisha's Nabarangpur district, stemming from a land dispute.

articles.headline: RAHUL REITERATES ‘TWO INDIAS’ PITCH, SLAMS BJP
articles.summary: Congress leader Rahul Gandhi reiterated his "two Indias" pitch, stating the country is divided between rich and poor, not by religion. He urged minority leaders to identify hate crime victims by religion.

articles.headline: WOMAN RAPED, KILLED IN J’KHAND, THREE ARRESTED
articles.summary: A woman, missing for six days, was found raped and murdered in Jharkhand’s Deoghar district. Police arrested three persons, including a 29-year-old woman, in connection with the crime.

articles.headline: 10-yr-old raped, killed in TN, 2 arrested; CM vows strict action
articles.summary: A 10-year-old girl was raped and killed in Coimbatore, Tamil Nadu. Police arrested K Karthik (33) and R Mohan (30). CM C Joseph Vijay vowed strict action, expressing outrage over the "horrific" incident.

articles.headline: ‘Work in progress’, says Wangchuk after MHA meet with civil bodies
articles.summary: Environmentalist Sonam Wangchuk expressed hope for constitutional safeguards for Ladakh under Article 371 after "in-principle understanding" was reached in MHA talks. He also voiced support for the viral "Cockroach Janata Party."

articles.headline: India’s global tie-ups are creating new avenues: PM
articles.summary: PM Narendra Modi stated India's growing global partnerships in technology, manufacturing, and clean energy are creating new employment opportunities. He distributed 51,000 appointment letters, highlighting pacts with countries like Netherlands and Sweden.

articles.headline: UP CONG CHIEF BOOKED OVER REMARKS AGAINST PM AT MAHOBA
articles.summary: Uttar Pradesh Congress chief Ajay Rai and supporters were booked for allegedly insulting PM Narendra Modi at a Mahoba event, causing obstruction and using "abusive language." Rai called the accusations fake and AI-generated.

articles.headline: Centre reviews VB-G RAM-G rollout preparedness
articles.summary: The Ministry of Rural Development reviewed states' preparedness for the July 1 rollout of the Viksit Bharat–Guarantee for Rozgar and Ajeevika Mission (Gramin) Act, 2025, which replaces MGNREGS and raises statutory wage employment.

articles.headline: ED arrests Pb realtor in land fraud case
articles.summary: The Enforcement Directorate arrested Punjab realtor Ajay Sehgal in a money laundering case for allegedly using "fake" consent letters to obtain Change of Land Use for a mega real estate project. Assets worth ₹100 crore were linked to him.

articles.headline: Cracks develop on bridge in Bihar after pillar sinks under its weight
articles.summary: Authorities barred heavy traffic on the 206.72-metre Jhamta Mahishakol bridge in Bihar after a pillar subsided. Built in 2022 for ₹7.32 crore, it's the second significant bridge damage in the state in less than a month.

articles.headline: Azam Khan’s jail term extended to 10 years
articles.summary: An MP-MLA sessions court in Rampur extended Samajwadi Party leader Azam Khan's prison sentence from seven to 10 years in a case involving alleged use of fake PAN cards. His son's seven-year term was maintained.

articles.headline: Rush at Rohtang Pass after fresh snow
articles.summary: Vehicles traversed fresh snowfall at the Rohtang Pass in Himachal Pradesh on Saturday, attracting tourists and locals to the scenic mountain pass.

articles.headline: In a first, four women set to head Indian high courts
articles.summary: For the first time, four high courts will simultaneously have women chief justices after the Supreme Court collegium recommended Justice Meenakshi M Rai for Patna High Court. This marks a milestone for women's representation in the judiciary.

articles.headline: Iran says army rebuilt in ceasefire
articles.summary: Iran's chief negotiator Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf warned of a "crushing" response if Washington resumes hostilities. He stated Iran's armed forces rebuilt during the ceasefire, dismissing US "excessive demands" in Pakistan-mediated talks.

articles.headline: Iran praises Tulsi Gabbard’s work
articles.summary: The Iranian Embassy in Armenia lauded Tulsi Gabbard for speaking 'truths' about Iran that US President Donald Trump disliked, following her resignation as Director of National Intelligence.

articles.headline: Starship soars
articles.summary: SpaceX completed a successful test flight of its largest Starship rocket, deploying mock satellites and executing a controlled splashdown in the Indian Ocean.

articles.headline: Ebola toll rises, more African countries at risk
articles.summary: Uganda confirmed three new Ebola cases, and the Red Cross reported three volunteer deaths in DRC. WHO declared it an international emergency, with 10 African countries at risk due to high mobility and insecurity.

articles.headline: China to send its 1st Hong Kong astronaut
articles.summary: China plans to launch its next manned mission to the Tiangong space station on Sunday, including Hong Kong's Li Jiaying among the Shenzhou-23 crew. This marks the country's first space mission with a Hong Kong astronaut.

articles.headline: IRGC-linked operative wanted to kill Ivanka
articles.summary: An Iraqi operative, Mohammad Baqer Saad Dawood Al-Saadi, allegedly plotted to assassinate Ivanka Trump in revenge for the 2020 US drone strike that killed Iranian commander Qasem Soleimani.

articles.headline: Hondius captain to leave hantavirus ship
articles.summary: The captain of the cruise ship MV Hondius, which was hit by a deadly hantavirus outbreak, was finally due to leave the vessel on Saturday, WHO chief said.

articles.headline: Many injured in head-on German tram collision
articles.summary: Two trams collided head-on in Duesseldorf, Germany, injuring over 50 people. The accident happened at a busy intersection; 28 were hospitalized, and the cause is unclear.

articles.headline: Climate change threatens global plant species as habitats diminish
articles.summary: Climate change threatens 18% of the world's known vascular plants, with 7-16% facing over 90% habitat loss by century's end. Researchers found shrinking suitable combinations of conditions for species, impacting conservation.

articles.headline: Himalayan salamanders: a creature fighting to survive
articles.summary: Conservation biologist Barkha Subba won a Whitley Award for her efforts to save the vulnerable Himalayan salamander. With only 5,000 left in India, wetlands loss and climate change threaten the species, which is crucial for ecosystem health.

articles.headline: Red-hot Sinner ready to rule
articles.summary: Jannik Sinner enters the French Open as world No.1, with an unbeaten run of 29 matches and five Masters titles, including three on clay. With Carlos Alcaraz injured, he is the runaway favorite, despite the French Open being his least productive Slam.

articles.headline: Pragg holds Giri, Keymer wins title
articles.summary: GM Praggnanandhaa drew against Anish Giri, while Vincent Keymer beat Jorden Van Foreest to win the Super Chess Classic title.

articles.headline: ‘WC broadcast near finalisation’
articles.summary: Former AIFF general secretary Shaji Prabhakaran stated that negotiations for the FIFA World Cup 2026 broadcast rights in India are complete, with an announcement expected soon.

articles.headline: Russell clinches Montreal sprint
articles.summary: George Russell won the Canadian Grand Prix sprint from pole position, with Kimi Antonelli finishing third amidst tensions between Mercedes teammates.

articles.headline: Fernandes player of PL season
articles.summary: Manchester United midfielder Bruno Fernandes was named Premier League Player of the Season.

articles.headline: AIFF adopts sports Governance Act
articles.summary: The All India Football Federation (AIFF) adopted the National Sports Governance Act on Saturday.

articles.headline: The Gurnoor-Auqib debate and priorities for selection
articles.summary: National selectors prioritized Gurnoor Brar's potential (height, pace) for the Afghanistan Test over Auqib Nabi's strong Ranji Trophy red-ball performance. This highlights attributes-based selection over domestic numbers, prompting debate on Ranji's value.

articles.headline: Shreyas hands PBKS win, keeps playoffs hope alive
articles.summary: Skipper Shreyas Iyer's unbeaten 101* led Punjab Kings to a seven-wicket win over Lucknow Super Giants, ending their losing streak and keeping playoff hopes alive with 15 points. Prabhsimran Singh scored 69.

articles.headline: BUMRAH LOST SHARPNESS DUE TO A NIGGLE, SAYS JAYAWARDENE
articles.summary: MI head coach Mahela Jayawardene revealed Jasprit Bumrah's reduced sharpness in early IPL games was due to a "slight niggle" from the T20 World Cup, affecting his pace and execution.

articles.headline: HDFC Bank rated 'Accumulate' despite underperformance and chairman's exit
articles.summary: HDFC Bank, rated 'Accumulate', trades at 2x book value despite underperformance since July '24. Chairman's resignation concerns are offset by strong financials, with target price ₹830. FY26 RoA projected at 1.9%, GNPA at 1.2%.

articles.headline: Petrol, diesel, CNG prices rise for third time in 10 days
articles.summary: Petrol, diesel, and CNG prices have increased for the third time in 10 days, reaching May 2022 highs. OMCs raised prices to offset rising input gas costs and a stronger US dollar, despite still incurring losses of ₹8-10/litre.

articles.headline: US Secretary Rubio meets PM Modi, discusses trade, energy, and White House invite
articles.summary: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio met PM Modi to discuss trade, energy security, and critical tech. Rubio invited Modi to the White House and announced an 'America First' visa tool. They also discussed West Asia and Indo-Pacific strategy.

articles.headline: Nifty 50 and Bank indices face resistance; bearishness easing
articles.summary: Nifty 50 and Nifty Bank show easing bearishness with marginal gains. Derivatives data suggests cautious optimism, though FIIs hold net short positions. Key resistances at 23,950-24,050 (Nifty) and 54,600-55,150 (Nifty Bank) need breach for bullish reversal.

articles.headline: Advised to exit Zee Entertainment & CarTradeTech amid downtrend risks
articles.summary: Investors are advised to exit Zee Entertainment Enterprises and CarTradeTech due to multi-year downtrends and no bullish reversal signs. Significant losses incurred, with further falls possible. Exits are recommended to mitigate further risk.

articles.headline: Supreme Court hears arguments on same-sex marriage rights petitions
articles.summary: The Supreme Court continues hearings on petitions seeking legal recognition for same-sex marriage, examining arguments on constitutional rights to equality and liberty. The verdict is anticipated to have significant implications for LGBTQ+ rights.

articles.headline: IOC selects new host city and announces Olympic qualification standards
articles.summary: The International Olympic Committee (IOC) has chosen a new host city for a future Olympic Games and announced revised qualification standards for athletes. These decisions aim to promote global sports and ensure high-level competition.

articles.headline: Delhi HC allows Vinesh Phogat to contest Asian Games trials, criticizes WFI
articles.summary: The Delhi High Court allowed Vinesh Phogat to participate in Asian Games trials, deeming WFI's selection policy 'vindictive' and exclusionary. Trials will be video-recorded with independent observers present.

articles.headline: Athletics records shattered: Gurindervir, Vishal, Tejaswin shine
articles.summary: Gurindervir Singh set a new 100m national record (10.09s), Vishal TK ran 400m under 45s (44.98s), and Tejaswin Shankar broke his decathlon record with 8057 points. These achievements mark significant milestones in Indian athletics.

articles.headline: Mohun Bagan withdraws players from India squad for Unity Cup
articles.summary: Mohun Bagan Super Giant pulled all seven players from the Indian squad for the Unity Cup in London, citing past AIFF's refusal to compensate for injuries outside FIFA windows.

articles.headline: Iran-US talks show progress towards ceasefire, Pakistan Army chief visits Tehran
articles.summary: US and Iran are nearing a memorandum of understanding to end the war, with talks showing "narrowing differences." Pakistan's army chief held discussions in Tehran amid hopes for a truce.

articles.headline: Supreme Court to hear MP dowry death case; actor-model's unnatural death probed
articles.summary: The Supreme Court will hear a suo motu case on the unnatural death of actor-model Twisha Sharma in Madhya Pradesh, amid scrutiny of the probe and her husband's police custody.

articles.headline: Row over Kerala poll chief's appointment as CM's secretary
articles.summary: Kerala's former Chief Electoral Officer's appointment as CM's secretary has sparked a political row, with BJP and CPI(M) alleging hypocrisy and planned interference, while Congress defends the decision.

articles.headline: US immigration agency clarifies green card application process tweak
articles.summary: The US immigration agency clarified its memo, stating workers demonstrating economic benefit or national interest will likely continue applying for green cards from within America, reaffirming existing law.

articles.headline: 82 killed in China's Shanxi province coal mine gas explosion
articles.summary: A gas explosion at a coal mine in China's Shanxi province killed at least 82 people, marking the country's deadliest mining accident in 17 years. Investigations into "serious illegal violations" are underway.

articles.headline: ITBP jawans protest at police HQ over alleged medical negligence
articles.summary: Around 50 armed ITBP personnel protested at Kanpur police headquarters, demanding action over alleged medical negligence that led to a jawan's mother losing her arm. A new inquiry committee was formed.

articles.headline: 16 deaths in Telangana from suspected heatstroke; ₹4 lakh ex-gratia announced
articles.summary: At least 16 people have died from suspected heatstroke in Telangana this summer, with temperatures exceeding 45°C. The state government announced ₹4 lakh ex-gratia for affected families.

articles.headline: BRS leader found murdered and mutilated in Telangana; rivalry suspected
articles.summary: A local BRS leader was found dead and mutilated in Telangana's Suryapet district. Police suspect old rivalries, as the deceased was a key witness in a past murder case.

articles.headline: Telangana Deputy Collector arrested for disproportionate assets over ₹100 crore
articles.summary: Telangana's Anti-Corruption Bureau arrested special grade deputy collector Marri Vamsi Mohan, revealing assets exceeding ₹100 crore. He was remanded to 14 days judicial custody.
